_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in using forensic tool suites (e.g., EnCase, Sleuthkit, FTK).
reference_document_element_description: Skill in preparing regulatory examination evidence packages, including control narratives, policy documentation, audit trails, and supporting artifacts that demonstrate compliance to examiner standards.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI RC domain. RC-S-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-S-006 (DQ, cosine: 0.487), AB-S-008 (AB, cosine: 0.479).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.8/10 (raw 0.485). Shared vocabulary: [skill]. DCWF-only terms [encase, forensic, ftk, sleuthkit, suites, tool] vs WIDAI-only terms [artifacts, audit, compliance, control, demonstrate, documentation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.845 (P=0.841, R=0.848). Balanced P/R — the 10-word DCWF element and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.9%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Skill in using forensic tool suites (e.g., EnCase, Sleuthkit, FTK)." and "Skill in preparing regulatory examination evidence packages, including control n". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.498 ≈ STS 0.485 (gap 0.013). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [skill] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.2%. Shared tokens: [skill]. DCWF-only: [encase, forensic, ftk, sleuthkit, suites, tool]. WIDAI-only: [artifacts, audit, compliance, control, demonstrate, documentation].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.9% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
